reasoning: Sudan has been engulfed in a catastrophic civil war since April 2023. As of late January 2025, Sudan remained one of the 7 African Union signatories that had not yet ratified the AfCFTA agreement. Furthermore, because the Sudanese government lacks a functioning, unified legislative body capable of domestically codifying and promulgating international trade treaties, they face insurmountable structural barriers to doing so. With only 25 days remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, it is virtually impossible for Sudan to complete the ratification process. As such, the forecast is set at the minimum allowable boundary of 2%.
